metadata.summary: Visualize large time series data with plotly.py in NiceGUI

documentation:
# NicePlotly-Resampler

[![PyPI](https://img.shields.io/pypi/v/niceplotly-resampler?color=dark-green)](https://pypi.org/project/niceplotly-resampler/)
[![PyPI downloads](https://img.shields.io/pypi/dm/niceplotly-resampler?color=dark-green)](https://pypi.org/project/niceplotly-resampler/)
[![GitHub license](https://img.shields.io/github/license/Vidpic/niceplotly-resampler?color=orange)](https://github.com/Vidpic/niceplotly-resampler/blob/main/LICENSE)
[![GitHub issues](https://img.shields.io/github/issues/Vidpic/niceplotly-resampler?color=blue)](https://github.com/Vidpic/niceplotly-resampler/issues)

`NicePloty-Resampler` is a tool for integrating Plotly into NiceGUI, designed to handle large time series datasets efficiently. To achieve this, it dynamically downsamples data based on the current graph view, ensuring smooth user interactions like panning and zooming.

Instead of implementing its own downsampling algorithms, NicePloty-Resampler utilizes the advanced functionality of [tsdownsample](https://github.com/predict-idlab/tsdownsample), which offers highly optimized methods for selecting representative data points. This allows the library to provide exceptional performance while maintaining simplicity.

By default, NicePloty-Resampler uses the MinMaxLTTB method to reduce datasets to 1000 key points for visualization.

![example](https://github.com/Vidpic/niceplotly-resampler/blob/main/docs/example.gif)

## Install
```bash
pip install niceplotly-resampler
```

## Usage

```python
from nicegui import ui
import plotly.graph_objects as go; import numpy as np
from niceplotly_resampler import FigureResampler

x = np.arange(1_000_000)
noisy_sin = (3 + np.sin(x / 200) + np.random.randn(len(x)) / 10) * x / 1_000

fig = FigureResampler(go.Figure())
fig.add_trace(go.Scattergl(name='noisy sine', showlegend=True, x=x, y=noisy_sin))
fig.update_layout(title='Noisy sine wave example', template='plotly_dark', title_x=0.5)

with ui.row().classes('w-full h-full'):
    fig.show(options={"displayModeBar": False}).classes('w-full h-full')
ui.run()
```

methods.name: niceplotly_resampler.figure_resampler.FigureResampler.__init__
documentation:
A resampling Plotly Figure wrapper supporting dynamic updates and downsampling.

Args:
    figure: The Plotly figure to wrap.
    num_points: The number of points to display in the figure.
    downsampler: The downsampler to use for resampling.
    parallel: Whether to use parallel processing for downsampling.

methods.name: niceplotly_resampler.figure_resampler.FigureResampler.add_trace
documentation:
Add a trace to the figure with optional row and column specification.

Args:
    trace: The trace to add to the figure.
    row: The row of the subplot to add the trace to.
    col: The column of the subplot to add the trace to.
    **kwargs: Additional keyword arguments to pass to the trace.

Returns:
    go.Figure: The updated figure with the added trace.

methods.name: niceplotly_resampler.figure_resampler.FigureResampler.update_layout
documentation:
Update the layout of the figure with the provided arguments.

Args:
    **kwargs: Additional keyword arguments to pass to the figure layout.

methods.name: niceplotly_resampler.figure_resampler.FigureResampler.update
documentation:
Update the figure with the provided FigureResampler.

Args:
    figure_resampler: The FigureResampler to change the figure to.

methods.name: niceplotly_resampler.figure_resampler.FigureResampler.reset
documentation: Reset the figure by removing all traces.

methods.name: niceplotly_resampler.figure_resampler.FigureResampler.show
documentation:
Create or update the nicegui plot with the current figure.

Args:
    options: Additional options to pass to the plot

Returns:
    ui.plotly: The nicegui plot object.

methods.name: niceplotly_resampler.figure_resampler.FigureResampler._resample_all_traces
documentation: Resample all traces in the figure

methods.name: niceplotly_resampler.figure_resampler.FigureResampler._format_bin_size
documentation:
Format the bin size with appropriate units (k, M, G, etc.).

Args:
    bin_size: The bin size to format.

Returns:
    The formatted bin size as a string.

methods.name: niceplotly_resampler.figure_resampler.FigureResampler._axis_to_subplot
documentation:
Determine the subplot row and column from the axis name.

Args:
    axis_name: The name of the axis.

Returns:
    tuple: The row and column of the subplot.

functions.name: niceplotly_resampler.event_handlers._on_relayout
documentation:
Handle relayout events to update the x and y axis ranges.

Args:
    event: The plotly_relayout event.

functions.name: niceplotly_resampler.event_handlers._on_doubleclick
documentation:
Handle double-click events to reset the figure layout and resample all traces.

Args:
    event: The plotly_doubleclick event.
